conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I think we should use the Go programming language for our new service. It has great concurrency support.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Go is efficient and compiles to a single binary. I'll write the services.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: But our team knows Python better. Let's use FastAPI with Python. It's more productive.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: FastAPI has automatic OpenAPI docs. Python is definitely faster to develop in.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We need to handle many concurrent connections. Rust with Tokio is better for that. Let's use Rust.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Rust is memory-safe and fast. I'll learn the ownership model.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I've been looking at Elixir with Phoenix. It handles real-time connections exceptionally well. Let's use Elixir.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Elixir is great for WebSockets. I'll set up the Phoenix channels.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We need to integrate with existing Java microservices. Let's just use Java with Spring Boot. It's the most compatible.

conversation.speaker: assistant
conversation.text: Spring Boot is mature and has everything. I'll set up the dependency injection.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: This is exhausting. Let's use Go. It's the best balance of performance and simplicity. Our infrastructure team supports it well.

query: What programming language is the team using for the new backend service?
